name: Maximum Payload Packet
description: Largest valid packet with 256-byte maximum payload. Tests CRC algorithm with maximum input.
purpose: Boundary condition: verify algorithm correctness with maximum payload

verification.verification_procedure: Execute CRC16::Calculate([header + seq + cmd + len + payload_256bytes], 262) and record output

expected_complete_packet.description: Complete packet bytes including CRC and footer
expected_complete_packet.structure: Header(2) + Seq(1) + Cmd(1) + Len(2) + Payload(256) + CRC(2) + Footer(1) = 265 bytes total

real_world_scenario.description: Daemon requests full hardware status including all relays, fans, temps, and drive info
real_world_scenario.use_case: GET_ALL_STATUS command returns large response with comprehensive device state

testing_notes: Tests that CRC algorithm handles large data streams correctly
Tests that protocol layer can construct and parse maximum-size packets
Verify memory usage doesn't overflow with 256-byte payload
Integration test: firmware should accept this packet and process correctly
Performance: verify CRC computation completes within timeout window

payload_generation.note: Pattern chosen for simplicity and reproducibility. Any 256-byte sequence could be used.
